spatial_doa: Based on the audio cues, output the azimuth and elevation. Note: Azimuth 0° is front, positive left, range [-180°, 180°]; Elevation 0° is horizontal, positive up, range [-90°, 90°].
Locate the sound source. Definition: Azimuth (0°=front, +=left, range [-180, 180]), Elevation (0°=level, +=up, range [-90, 90]).
Determine the direction of arrival. Azimuth origin is straight ahead, positive to the left [-180, 180]. Elevation origin is eye level, positive upwards [-90, 90].
Provide precise coordinates. Coordinate system: Azimuth [-180, 180] (0 is front, left is positive); Elevation [-90, 90] (0 is horizontal, up is positive).
What are the azimuth and elevation angles? Recall that Azimuth 0 is front/positive left [-180, 180] and Elevation 0 is flat/positive up [-90, 90].
Estimate the spatial location. Use this scale: Azimuth [-180 to 180] (0=front, left+), Elevation [-90 to 90] (0=horizontal, up+).
Give me the numerical azimuth and elevation. Azimuth: 0 is front, positive left [-180, 180]. Elevation: 0 is horizontal, positive up [-90, 90].
Where is the sound coming from? Output precise values with Azimuth 0° at front (left +, range -180 to 180) and Elevation 0° at horizon (up +, range -90 to 90).
Analyze the DoA. System: Azimuth [-180, 180] (0=front, +left), Elevation [-90, 90] (0=level, +up).
Output the angle of the sound source. Define Azimuth: 0 is front, positive left, [-180, 180]. Define Elevation: 0 is horizontal, positive up, [-90, 90].
multi_spatial_doa: Based on the audio, determine the azimuth and elevation of the {gender} speaker.
Locate the {gender} speaker. Output azimuth and elevation.
Where is the {gender} voice coming from? Provide spatial coordinates.
Find the direction of the {gender} speaker in the overlapping audio.
Identify the spatial position of the {gender} speaker.
visual_grounding: Identify the 3D boxes for the {obj_category} in this scene. Follow this format: bbox_i=Bbox(category, x, y, z, dx, dy, dz)
Locate the {obj_category} in the room. Output their bounding boxes as bbox_i=Bbox(category, x, y, z, dx, dy, dz).
Please find the 3D bounding boxes for {obj_category}. The output format should be: bbox_i=Bbox(category, x, y, z, dx, dy, dz)
Detect the {obj_category} and provide their 3D coordinates. Use format: bbox_i=Bbox(category, x, y, z, dx, dy, dz)
Give me the 3D Bboxes for {obj_category}. Format: bbox_i=Bbox(category, x, y, z, dx, dy, dz)
